title: Function Overloading in C++

content.problemStatement:
Write a C++ program to:


Create a class Calculator
Overload function `add()` for:

Two integers
Two float numbers


Call both functions in `main()` and display result
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers a and b separated by a space. The second line contains two floating‑point numbers x and y separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with two numbers separated by a space. The first number is the integer sum a+b. The second number is the sum x+y printed with exactly three digits after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 10+20=30 and 1.2+3.4=4.6, formatted to three decimal places as 4.600.

solution.approach: The task only requires adding two integers and two floating‑point numbers for each test case. A small class Calculator is created with two overloaded add methods – one taking int parameters and returning int, the other taking float parameters and returning float. For every test case we read the four values, invoke the appropriate add method and print the results. The floating‑point sum is formatted to three decimal places using String.format. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
